title: Subtree of Another Tree
problem_statement: Check whether one binary tree is a subtree of another, meaning there exists a node in the larger tree whose subtree matches the smaller tree exactly.

examples.explanation: subRoot appears as a subtree of root.

constraints: The number of nodes is in the range [1, 2000]
intuition: For each candidate node in the main tree, compare the subtree rooted there with the target subtree using a structural equality check.

approaches.name: Traversal plus subtree equality
approaches.description: Traverse the main tree and for each node use a subtree comparison helper to determine if it matches subRoot.
approaches.why_not: Serialization and string search can reduce the constant factor but requires careful delimiter handling.
interview_commentary: Try every node in root as a possible subtree root.
Use subtree equality checks to validate each candidate.
Return early when a matching subtree is found.

interview_qa.q: What is the main idea of this solution?
interview_qa.a: Try every node in root as a candidate subtree root and compare structures with subRoot.

interview_qa.q: When do you compare the subtree?
interview_qa.a: When the current node value matches subRoot’s root value or for every node in the tree.
Because for each node in the larger tree you may compare an entire subtree with subRoot.
Whether both trees have identical structure and values at each node.
